rationale: The cited paper is one of the three discussant pieces that this note is explicitly written to answer, so it is not a passing reference. The citing paper repeatedly engages Alba and Cooke (2004) in core sections (clarifications, additional analyses, and model extensions), including rebutting specific conjectures with reported results and added correlation checks. It also uses their comments to motivate proposed extensions (e.g., alternative imputation rules and measurement issues). This is a meaningful argumentative role in framing and defending the citing paper's claims.
relation_description: The citing paper is a direct response to Alba and Cooke's critique of the BHH imputation model. It challenges several of their specific claims with empirical and conceptual counterarguments while incorporating some of their suggestions as avenues for model extension.
